questions.title: Hello World with Input
questions.description: Write a Java program that reads a name from input and prints 'Hello, [name]!' where [name] is the input value.
questions.inputFormat: A single string representing a name.
questions.constraints: Name length: 1 to 50 characters, only alphabets.
questions.explanation: Use Scanner class to read input. Use System.out.println to output the greeting message with the name.

questions.title: Integer Operations
questions.description: Write a Java program that reads two integers and performs addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division. Print each result on a separate line. For division, print only the integer quotient.
questions.inputFormat: Two space-separated integers A and B.
questions.outputFormat: Print four lines: sum, difference, product, and quotient (integer division).
questions.constraints: 1 <= A, B <= 1000, B will not be 0.
questions.explanation: Read two integers using Scanner. Perform arithmetic operations. Use integer division (/) for quotient.

questions.title: Even or Odd Check
questions.description: Write a Java program that reads an integer and determines if it's even or odd. Print 'Even' if the number is even, otherwise print 'Odd'.
questions.explanation: Use modulo operator (%) to check if a number is divisible by 2. If N % 2 == 0, it's even; otherwise, it's odd.
hints: Even numbers have remainder 0 when divided by 2

questions.title: Factorial Calculation
questions.description: Write a Java program that calculates the factorial of a given non-negative integer N. Factorial of N (N!) is the product of all positive integers from 1 to N. Note: 0! = 1.
questions.explanation: Use a loop to multiply numbers from 1 to N. Handle the special case of 0! = 1. Use long to handle larger results.
hints: Multiply result by each number from 1 to N
Use long to prevent overflow for larger factorials

questions.title: Palindrome Number Check
questions.description: Write a Java program that checks if a given integer is a palindrome. A palindrome number reads the same forwards and backwards. Print 'Palindrome' if it is, otherwise print 'Not Palindrome'. Negative numbers are not palindromes.
questions.explanation: Reverse the number and compare with original. Handle negative numbers as non-palindromes. Use mathematical operations to reverse digits.
hints: Build reversed number by multiplying by 10 and adding digit
